spec_match.rationale: The page demonstrates navigator.deviceMemory as a bucketed hint, Chrome 147 platform bucket limits, minimum and maximum clamping, and missing-API fallback behavior.

interactive.rationale: The tier picker has live controls for source mode, platform profile, raw memory simulation, fallback policy, and scenario buttons with immediate readout updates.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The concept now maps Device Memory API buckets to concrete image, video, prefetch, cache, and background-task quality outcomes.

edge_cases.rationale: The page visibly covers low-memory clamping, maximum bucket clamping, legacy/nonmatching live buckets, and the no-navigator.deviceMemory fallback path.

references.rationale: The references section links directly to https://chromestatus.com/feature/6330376953921536.

design_system.rationale: The page uses shared CSS variables, keeps local button background and color overrides paired, and remains an interactive demo rather than a static code card.

summary: The critique is addressed: the page now includes the required ChromeStatus anchor and a richer interactive tier picker that handles Chrome 147 bucket limits, fallback policy, and visible outcomes.
